name: Coffee Coffeecake with Espresso Glaze
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Instant espresso dissolved in 1 tablespoon Hot water
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, sift together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
2. In another bowl with an electric mixer, cream the butter and add the sugar gradually, beating until light and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Beat in the vanilla extract.
3. Alternately add the flour mixture (beginning and ending with flour) and sour cream to the butter mixture, blending after each addition.
4. Transfer about a third of the batter to another bowl and stir in the espresso mixture until well combined.
5. Spoon half of the plain batter into a well-buttered 8-inch bundt pan, spreading it evenly. Spread the coffee batter over the plain batter, then spread the remaining plain batter on top.
6. Bake the cake in the center of a preheated 350°F oven for 55-60 minutes, or until golden and a tester inserted into the center comes out clean. Let cool in the pan on a rack for 30 minutes.
7. Invert the cake onto the rack and let it cool completely.
8. In a bowl, stir together 2 tablespoons brewed coffee and espresso powder, stirring until the powder dissolves. Sift the confectioner's sugar and add it, stirring until well combined. If necessary, add more coffee to achieve a pourable consistency.
9. Pour the glaze over the coffeecake and let the cake stand for 10 minutes or until the glaze has set. Serve warm.
